TEXT.English: [H1]AdvCiv to AdvCiv-SAS Core Changes[\H1][PARAGRAPH:2]Non-exhaustive list of changes in AdvCiv-SAS as compared to AdvCiv (excluding balance changes, for that please refer to the Sevopedia entries of the items you want to see, for example "Units", "Technologies", or "Traits" to have the latest updated version of their effects.)[PARAGRAPH:1]For more details, in particular about the changes, please visit:[NEWLINE]- if online, https://github.com/wonderingabout/AdvCiv-SAS/, in particular the "Quick Start Guide" there (also appended/included as a text .md file in AdvCiv-SAS). If you want to see the full very exhaustive code changes between AdvCiv current latest stable, for example 1.12 here, and AdvCiv-SAS, please visit this pull request compare: https://github.com/wonderingabout/AdvCiv-SAS/pull/13 . Be warned though it can be very lengthy, so read rather the quick start guide mentionned above preferably for the shorter and less exhaustive version if you'd prefer.[NEWLINE]- if offline: you can also view most (almost all) of the information above by navigating the docs in your AdvCiv-SAS folder (for example if you have the Steam version of the game,it would be here (else adapt to where your civ4 and advciv-sas install paths are): "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\"), and opening them with a text editor (i recommend Notepad++, but the default Windows Notepad.exe can also open .txt and .md files too just with less display features (but still all info unless i'm mistaken anyways)).[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Translations[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- For the new content or modified one in this mod (AdvCiv-SAS), only English translations are provided. If your game is in another language, you will still see some text, but in English. For modders, see the detailed explanation (code comment), mostly provided by ChatGPT and which was very helpful (to me at least, anyways, ) on how this is implemented at TXT_KEY_ADVCIV_SAS_CORE_CHANGES_PEDIA_SR 's entry in Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\Assets\XML\Text\CIV4GameText_advc_pedia_Lengthy_Custom.xml[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Sevopedia[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- New feature in sevopedia leader: AI Personality Panels, that aim to represent each Leader's behavorial tendencies and preferences quite exhaustively, and their ranking of such tendencies vs what other leaders ((would) tend to do) anyways, as represented in their XML data, see for example [LINK=LEADER_GANDHI]Gandhi's leader page for example[\LINK]. Extensive documentation has been written as well to help you navigate these and understand how they work. Please read for more details the general introduction to such documents(ation(s?)) in Mods Info [LINK=CONCEPT_ADVCIV_SAS_CORE_AI_PERSONALITY_PANEL_INTRO].4_ADVCIV_SAS_CORE_AI_PERSONALITY_PANEL_INTRO[\LINK][NEWLINE]- Cleanup or adding more features in other Sevopedia categories, such as SevopediaReligion (add Leaders panel (based on History Rewritten's code (modified for AdvCiv-SAS)) and Buildings panel too (based on RFC DOC's code modified for AdvCiv-SAS too anyways etc)), or the SevopediaLeader in general (reordered the size and position of the other panels in SevopediaLeader with or not with other mods's code, or/and other Sevopedia Categories too).[NEWLINE]- Expanded Sevopedia so it uses all screen space available (no margins anymore), now more space to display info if want/need or visual clarity, as some also do for example or/and comparison anyways/etc[NEWLINE]- Reduced main categories width from approxiamtely 200px to 124px approximately, this quite greatly increases the space of the Sevopedia for the content or items category(?)/column (the one that lists for example for the leaders category "Alexander[...]", "Ashoka", etc.. until the end of alphabet and for all other main categories too that have a listing of items (which is almost all if i'm not mistaken anyways)). Hopefully clearer now for this non critical info, while still preserving the 2nd category's width, using shortened names when possible or necessary in 1st/main categories's 1st column.[NEWLINE]- Added a few or many or at least some wikipedia real entries or from some other source (see code comments or/and docs for the info if it is, but as a general rule unless specified otherwise most if not all but at least most pedia type of content comes from wikipedia and generally in english (version of it(i.e. of wikipedia)), thanks wikipedia, anyways etc, for example the [LINK=RELIGION_BUDDHISM]Buddhism[\LINK][NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Renaming[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- "We fear you are becoming too advanced" (WFYABTA) has been renamed to "We fear you are trading more than us" (WFYATMTU), as WFYABTA is misleading, and not related at all to your tech progress but to how much you trade if i'm not mistaken, and you can get WFYABTA even if you are lagging behind in tech progress, hopefully clearer now.[NEWLINE]- (Air) interception chance for nukes is now named "Adjusted Air Interception Chance", as it also accounts for Nuke's Air evasion chance if i am not mistaken, see also this for details: https://forums.civfanatics.com/threads/sdi-icbm-and-tactical-nukes.239415/ [NEWLINE]- Renamed [LINK=TERRAIN_SNOW]"Ice" (Terrain) to "Ice Sheet"[\LINK], and [LINK=FEATURE_ICE]"Ice" (Feature) to "Ice Cap"[\LINK], hopefully clearer and more accurate, see their Sevopedia entries for details.[NEWLINE]- Renamed "Ressource" to "Bonus" for shorter Sevopedia category width, but also perhaps a good opportunity to match code name and game name maybe at least is how i would want to do it an AdvCiv-SAS if hopefully fine as in functionning well anyways etc.[NEWLINE]- Renamed [LINK=BONUS_GRAPES]"Wine" (Bonus) to "Grapes"[\LINK], see below at "Balance" changes in AdvCiv-SAS for details.[NEWLINE]- Renamed [LINK=BONUS_CATTLE]"Cow" (Ressource/Bonus) to "Cattle"[\LINK], and renamed [LINK=BONUS_ELEPHANTS]"Ivory" (Ressource) to "Elephants"[\LINK], hopefully clearer and more accurate (and immersive maybe), see the Sevopedia entry for details.[NEWLINE]- Renamed Religion "Taoism" to "Daoism" and all related entires (units, buildings, descriptions/history, etc), see the Sevopedia page for details or example(s) [LINK=RELIGION_DAOISM]"Daoism"[\LINK] or project/mod docs for more details[NEWLINE]- Renamed "great wonders" to "world wonders", but functionally the exact same if i am not mistaken, i hope it is clearer, more intutiive, and in line with "world project", hinting more strongly at the fact that only one is possible in the entire world if i am not mistaken, anyways etc.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Rules[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- All units now are affected by city defenses (walls, castles, etc if any more/other), even gunpowder and later on units, seems more realistic to me this way, as walls can help against guns and against all (almost?) units, actually even if not having the tech for guns itself. See also for details: bIgnoreBuildingDefense in CIV4UnitInfos.xml or/and (tranlslate to english ((following) web page (anyways etc)) using web browser or such) https://gforestshade.github.io/kujira/post/civ4unitinfos/#bignorebuildingdefense for details (too anyways etc(.)) anyways etc... .[NEWLINE]- Air explore units (for example the [LINK=UNIT_AIRSHIP]Dirigible[\LINK] (old airship)) can move through all terrains, including peaks and water tiles.[NEWLINE]- Not tested if i may say but anyways etc, in theory AIs should value Iron bonus/ressource especially more, and horse more too, as an objective, using iAIObjective 10 (was 0), (ideally not restricting trade if uneeded extra in possession but not tested nor do i know too much about it anyways etc but hopefully maybe would work as intended maybe not guaranteed though anyways etc may or not anyways etc) if they don't have it, was 0.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Balance (non-exhaustive)[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- As part of renaming wine to grapes, additionally reworked grapes: now improved by [LINK=IMPROVEMENT_FARM]IMPROVEMENT_FARM[\LINK], not anymore by the old improvement_winery now removed. This matches historicality better if i may say and if it's a word anyways etc, as wine was consumed much before 4000 BC. Also, some wines are not made from grapes at all if i am not mistaken, and grapes can be eaten out of the wine usage. Updated bonus's description with a wikipedia based one as often if not always done when updating pedia entries of/in advciv-sas or and other mods using this mod's (i.e. this advciv-sas's code anyways etc). Also, the winery/happiness part of wine is transferred to the grocer building instead, now grapes gives happiness not health anymore. Similarly although not directly related, sugar now also gives happiness not health anymore through grocer. Hopefully buffes these mid game bonus effects while making these ressources more relevant as they were (a) bit (anyways etc) weak compared to say wheat or corn. See code comments for details or the [LINK=BONUS_GRAPES]ingame sevopedia entry of the grapes[\LINK] with also maybe or not or yes anyways etc latest updated info if anything changed since i wrote (am writing currently but anyways etc also wrote when viewing from later the new now maybe or not or yes anyways etc) but this should be the bulk of it and hopefully makes gameplay more accurate or/and interesting perhaps or/and enjoyable or not anyways etc.[NEWLINE]- Technically a balance change mixed with rules, but easier to put it all here for simplicity anyways etc: adjusted the name and repartition and spawn chance as well as yields of many ressources, please view sevopedia bonus for latest info and this commit (see README_Quick_Get_Started_Guide.md that also has the commit link in the docs of this mod, at bullet point mentioning content similar to this one you are reading here and now i mean if still updated or/and accurate here or there or/and other or and not anyways etc) for details. In general, to get the main idea of it, some ressources that were weak are now stronger (for example silver, [LINK=BONUS_DEER]deer[\LINK], etc.), some are a bit weaker (for example gold, but it could barely be considered a nerf anyways etc), ressources/bonuses overall give less gpt to make game more gradual. Food land ressources spawn significantly less but after various testing increased it to values where they still spawn enough for game not to be boring or tedious, the idea is there will be more compeititon around them, as a result they also generally have a bit stronger bonuses, as well as reworked ones (for example sugar gives base hammer (productivity, horse gives base food but then improved gives only hammer, etc, see sevopedia bonus for details)), and deer in particular spawns much more as it is the only representative of snow or rather tundra ressources, its bonuses are strongest which seems accurate as it is both a very healthy and rich food. As a result, tundra cities if they have deer near them should be a lot more viable, example that may not be accurate in the future of me writing this, but for example as of now these screenshots from the drive for example show how it looks (view around screenshot 5235 to 5249 of the google drive for other samples of that time of this rework now finalized as of me writing this anyways etc): view for example screenshots 5238 of the drive (see/view anyways etc docs of the mod (README.md and such for the link anyways etc) and 5241 of the drive as well for example (same) anyways etc). Also, water ressources spawn more, especially for the now renamed clam into [LINK=BONUS_MOLLUSCS]the more general molluscs[\LINK] and fish as they are general ressources. Similarly, i have run a few tests to make sure (or at least try to (as best as i could or and wanted anyways etc hopefully accurate enough or not or yes or and other or and not anyways etc)) they don't spawn too much, so these are quite conservative tweaks but significant. With also buildings like the [LINK=BUILDING_HARBOR]harbor[\LINK] quite strongly buffed (view sevopedia for details) now, water planting should be a strong alternative to purely land ones, especially to complete an otherwise nice land location but poorer in ressources. I tried to make sure naval planting doesn't become too op or the opposite for land, i hope more versatility and strategy(ies? Anyways etc) can be used now as part of this rework, as well as more accurate gameplay. On that note, some pedia entries have been updated, [LINK=BONUS_WHALE]whales[\LINK] have been buffed too, etc, please view for details sevopedia bonus or/and docs or/and other pages anyways etc (or don't blame me if not accurate info you have hehe if i may say but do as you prefer here is all the info i have or most that was not too tedious or forgetted or rather forgotten xd if i may say but anyways etc of me to say but anyways etc... I hope gameplay will or and can be more interesting now with these balance changes without skewing too much at one side or the other, and that now there are more possibilities to play civ4 in a balanced perhaps competitive way, at least in theory and to best i could do at least of now, hopefully enjoyable or and helpful or not at least to me, may be tweaked in the future or and not, anyways etc.[NEWLINE]- Made ressources/bonuses generally spawn more towards the edges of the map, it was a bit too skewed towards central spawning for most bonuses, giving central start too big of an advantage over edge start, hopefully and with other bonuses and changes to spawn rate and such a bit closer now or/and balanced anyways etc[NEWLINE]- Shrines now require to also have the corresponding religion as a prequirement to build them (and not only just the holy city as before, which is still required now), this means for example that if you conquer a holy city that doesn't have a shrine, you can't build the shrine until/unless you have the matching religion if i am not mistaken (for example to build the [LINK=BUILDING_DAOIST_SHRINE]Daoist Shrine (Currently named "The Dai Miao" as of now (i.e. (as of?) me writing this (sevopedia) doc) anyways etc)[\LINK] you need to have the [LINK=RELIGION_DAOISM]"Daoism"[\LINK] religion too. This allows to display shrines in sevopedia and perhaps also makes more sense in terms of gameplay if it doesn't create issues hopefully, trying it to see anyways etc, see sevopedia entries or/and project/mod docs for details[NEWLINE]- Removed religion "confucianism", added [LINK=RELIGION_PAGANISM]religion "paganism"[\LINK], more popular and relevant for civ4 maybe as many leaders were religious if i am not mistaken but with no religion, see notes_about_religious_design.txt for details in the docs online on github or(/and(anyways etc)) offline in this project files too for details[NEWLINE]- Fixed and ajusted many religious inaccuracies of favourite religions, at least those i spotted with (being) certainty (certain enough) maybe anwyas etc (or not or/and other or/and not anways etc), for example frederick the great was agnostic not christian, brennus more leaning non religous than pagan (not inaccurate since paganism didn't exist but may fix other xml related fields or maybe not such as religion preference or not anwyays etc), and genghis khan, while tengrist something and not strictly daoist, expressed or/and showed(?) a strong respect(/liking?) for (a(/the?)) daoist leader, so his favourite religion can be assessed to be daoism, especially if he had to choose between civ4 only religions, here being none or daoism, he leans more strongly -much mroe if i am not mistake but anyways etc) towrds daoism), so a leader can have a favourite religion without it being their religion of themselves, see notes mentionned above for details, and this is all if i am not mistaken, but (i thnk)(and hope but anyways etc) if this is (and i was) accurate enouh (but anyways etc) hopefuly accurate enough and (much maybe(?) maybe or not or/and other or not anyways etc) accurate now hopefully maybe or not or/and other or/and not (but) anwyays etc[NEWLINE]- Barbarians are now much stronger in the early game economically, they should now grow at a similar pace than human and other players too until the middle game where they will gradually fade. They should also spawn cities more often and be relevant longer in the game, even though for now at least if not always or not they have trouble to grow past a certain size, and are still not a real player (no diplomacy and scattered cities that are independent, but that contribute together to the "barbarian(s?)((,)'s) civilization"), and become a key part in politics or invasion decisions, maybe i mean or not or/and other or not anyways etc. Their (now new)(anyways etc) overall strength increase (for example they are also harder to kill at all difficulties (less to no combat bonus against them, but never reverse bonus to them to make the game fair and balanced and fun as much as possible at least to me or and maybe (to) others ideally or maybe not (and or maybe not ideally or not or other or and not anyways etc) but anwyays etc) is gradual with difficulties), and they should not be (too) op though so hopefully not overbearing either but demanding more strategy or/and attention to hopefully make the game interesting, while filling some unsettled areas if not claimed fast enough in a quite realistic way too maybe anyways etc. They are also more calculating in who they attack, may raze cities much more than in base AdvCiv/Civ4 if am not mistaken anyways, and are more calculating in who they attack target the weak not the strong, anyways etc, or/and other changes or and not anyways etc. See [LINK=CIVILIZATION_BARBARIAN]Barbarians (Civilization)[\LINK] for details ingame or/and the XML for example for the Steam version of the game (adjust to where your AdvCiv-SAS mod is if path is different or/and you don't use the Steam version of the game) C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\Assets\XML\Civilizations\CIV4CivilizationInfos.xml and related files like CIV4BuildingInfos.xml and CIV4HandicapInfo.xml or/and such.[NEWLINE][NEWLINE][BOLD]Fixes[\BOLD][NEWLINE]- Fixed a seemingly AdvCiv/Civ 4 bug where Gandhi's nowar attitude prob were pleased(110) / pleased(115) instead of (seemingly how should be if i am not mistaken anyways etc) pleased(110) / friendly(115)[NEWLINE]- (Seemingly now) Fixed a seemingly bug in base AdvCiv/Civ4 where barbarians could build shwedagon paya or mausolleum of mausollos for example (maybe other wonders too, and national wonders too like moai and hermitage etc) as happened in auto play, sometimes even completing them, by using iBuildUnitProb 100 or a similar high value (was 0 in base AdvCiv code or/and civ4 code anyways etc but anyways etc), seems to be extremely effective, testing more to be sure. Note: I had(have? Anyways etc) tried other approaches like iWonderConstructRand -9999 on LEADER_BARBARIAN in leader head infos xml file, or FLAVOR_MILITARY 10 but did not work at all it seems. I also removed all old logic of buildingclass NONE that seemed to not have any effect on wonder building whether it was here (national wonders) or not (removed it for world wonders, but sitll exact same results it seems of wonders being built, sometimes completed even anyways etc), maybe such barbarian wonder building behaviour/tendency/possibility was just suppressed because barbarians did not grow fast enough to support as in encourage as in do wonder building (activites) anyways etc, but now that they grow fast enough at human and other player's pace, they can make wonders or attempt to, which "derails" from their original warmongering goal and intent in AdvCiv-SAS but anyways, so refraining them from doing it, anyways etc anyways etc...[NEWLINE]- Fixed a seemingly AdvCiv/Civ 4 bug of Sevopedia Unit's placeRequires's Religion button (for example any religious missionary unit) not redirecting to sevopedia religion (nothing happens on click anyways etc), by replacing, in sevopediaunit py file, in placeRequires function/method (of this file anyways etc), WidgetTypes.WIDGET_HELP_RELIGION with WidgetTypes.WIDGET_PEDIA_JUMP_TO_RELIGION(,) as is done already by base advciv and successfully in sevopedia building, anyways etc anyways etc anyways etc

[TAB]An exhaustive/extensive documentation has been written about the AI Personality Panel feature (that is) in AdvCiv-SAS (in the SevopediaLeader category), explaining, in broad, general ways how it works and how to read the data from a new player's perspective.[PARAGRAPH:2]It is recommended to read it to understand better how it works.[PARAGRAPH:2]You can view it:[NEWLINE]- online on github (todo add link),[NEWLINE]- or even if you are offline, you can read the .md files as any text file, for example with Notepad++ (or the default Notepad.exe of windows too if you prefer but Notepad.exe has less advanced features, still more than good enough for reading a doc especially if you prefer and should display all info as well if i'm not mistaken, anyways etc anyways). The file path is, for example if you have the Steam version of Civ4 (adapt to where your game and mod is if you use a non-Steam (or other than Steam) version (maybe rather anyways): "C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Sid Meier's Civilization IV Beyond the Sword\Beyond the Sword\Mods\AdvCiv-SAS\README_AI_Personality_Panel.md")[PARAGRAPH:2]You'll get most info from there hopefully rather than me copy pasting the same doc (painfully hehe but that i would gladly do (maybe or not or other etc anyways) but better avoid if possible anyways) in sevopedia ingame and it being potentially outdated after not long, etc anyways, thanks.
